info.title: ssh-cp
info.description: .NET tool to copy files between hosts using SSH/SFTP

hookCapture.patchTarget: Parse-postfix (OK: Command.Parse, OK: Command.Parse, OK: CommandLineConfiguration.Parse, OK: CommandLineConfiguration.Parse, OK: CommandLineParser.Parse, OK: CommandLineParser.Parse, OK: CommandLineConfiguration.Invoke, OK: CommandLineConfiguration.Invoke, OK: ParseResult.Invoke, OK: ParseErrorAction.Invoke, OK: HelpAction.Invoke, OK: CommandLineConfiguration.InvokeAsync, OK: CommandLineConfiguration.InvokeAsync, OK: ParseResult.InvokeAsync, OK: RootCommand.ctor(String))

x-inspectra.cliParsedDescription: Copy files between hosts using SSH/SFTP

options.name: -n
options.description: Treat the destination as a path including the filename/directory name (for renaming)

options.name: --ssh-option
options.description:
Set an SSH Config option, for example: Ciphers=chacha20-poly1305@openssh.com
Supported options: Hostname, User, Port, ConnectTimeout, GlobalKnownHostsFile, UserKnownHostsFile, HashKnownHosts, StrictHostKeyChecking, PreferredAuthentications, PubkeyAuthentication, IdentityFile, GSSAPIAuthentication, GSSAPIDelegateCredentials, GSSAPIServerIdentity, RequiredRSASize, SendEnv, Ciphers, HostKeyAlgorithms, KexAlgorithms, MACs, PubkeyAcceptedAlgorithms, TCPKeepAlive, ServerAliveCountMax, ServerAliveInterval, IdentitiesOnly, ProxyJump, CASignatureAlgorithms, CertificateFile, PasswordAuthentication, BatchMode

arguments.name: source
arguments.description:
Source path(s)
- To copy a file or directory, leave the source path without a trailing '/'
- To copy the *contents* of a directory, add a trailing '/' to the path
To copy from a remote system, prefix the path with '<ssh destination>:'. For example: 'user@host.com:/home/user/file'

arguments.name: destination
arguments.description:
Destination path
- When the '-n' option is *not* specified, the directory path to copy to.
- When the '-n' option is specified, the path includes the target name for the file or directory
  This allows renaming the file or directory. Only a single source can be specified
The destination directory and its parents are created if they don’t exist
To copy to a remote system, prefix the path with '<ssh destination>:'. For example: 'user@host.com:/tmp'
